headlines.title: E Jean Carroll demands Donald Trump pay $5.8m in damages from 2019 case
headlines.snippet: E Jean Carroll is seeking $5.8 million in damages from Donald Trump related to a 2019 case. Her lawyers want the US Supreme Court to expedite the payment resolution after it declined an appeal.

headlines.title: US signs $1 lease with Israel to build permanent embassy in West Jerusalem
headlines.snippet: The US and Israel have signed a $1 lease for land to construct a permanent US embassy in West Jerusalem.

headlines.title: US Supreme Court hands wealthy donors more sway with latest decision
headlines.snippet: A US Supreme Court decision is criticized for potentially increasing the influence of wealthy donors and special interests in politics, with some critics suggesting it invites corruption.

headlines.title: Iran to open ‘communication channel’ on MoU with US after talks in Qatar
headlines.snippet: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister stated that part of $6 billion in frozen assets would be used to purchase needed goods. This follows talks in Qatar concerning a memorandum of understanding with the US.

headlines.title: Russian couple arrested after climbing Empire State Building
headlines.snippet: Two Russian climbers were arrested after ascending the Empire State Building in New York. Their climb was reportedly part of a marriage proposal.

headlines.title: Oil prices fall as US leaders strike positive note on Iran talks
headlines.snippet: Oil prices dropped by over 1 percent, reaching their lowest levels since March, following positive comments from US leaders regarding talks with Iran. Brent futures fell to $71.57 a barrel and US West Texas Intermediate crude to $68.58.

headlines.title: Iranian parliament speaker says IAEA barred from bombed nuclear sites
headlines.snippet: Iran's parliament speaker stated that the IAEA is not permitted to inspect bombed nuclear sites. Inspections are reportedly limited to two sites, including the Bushehr power plant, and are not allowed under any circumstances for damaged facilities.

headlines.title: Centcom leads regional security dialogue with Middle East military officials
headlines.snippet: US Central Command led a regional security dialogue in Bahrain with military officials from ten Middle Eastern countries. Discussions focused on enhancing defense collaboration and underscored a shared commitment to regional security and stability.

headlines.title: RSF committed crimes against humanity in Sudan's el-Fasher, Amnesty says
headlines.snippet: Amnesty International has concluded that the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) committed crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing in el-Fasher, Sudan. The report details the deliberate recruitment, rape, and killing of children by the RSF.

headlines.title: Israeli army detonates large explosion in southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: The Israeli army detonated a large explosion near Rashaf in southern Lebanon, in the Bint Jbeil district. Bombings were also reported in Beit Yahoun.

headlines.title: Russian strikes in Ukraine kill six, wound dozens
headlines.snippet: Russian strikes in Ukraine resulted in six deaths and dozens of injuries.

headlines.title: Lebanon, Syria join CENTCOM-led Middle East security dialogue for first time
headlines.snippet: For the first time, Lebanon and Syria participated in the CENTCOM-led Middle East security dialogue, engaging with military officials from across the region.

headlines.snippet: The giant container ship named 'Notre-Dame' has arrived in France.

headlines.snippet: The US has signed an agreement to construct a permanent embassy in Jerusalem.

headlines.title: Attackers target homes of Greek ruling party figures, killing one

headlines.title: Turkey, Iraq discuss cooperation ahead of pipeline deal expiry
headlines.snippet: Turkey and Iraq are discussing cooperation regarding the Iraq-Turkey Crude Oil Pipeline ahead of its contract expiration.

headlines.title: Body tasked with Gaza governance says Cyprus talks 'highly productive'
headlines.snippet: A committee responsible for overseeing post-war Gaza concluded two days of productive talks in Cyprus. The discussions focused on alleviating suffering and reconstruction efforts.

headlines.snippet: Mohamed Salah is racing against time to recover from a strain for Egypt's upcoming match against Australia.

headlines.title: Iran prevents transit of over 3,600 kilograms of methamphetamine to Europe

headlines.title: Iran warns UN of Israel’s ‘deliberate’ and ‘public’ threat against Leader of Islamic Revolution

headlines.title: Pezeshkian: Government would have fully complied if Leader had ordered not to negotiate
headlines.snippet: President Masoud Pezeshkian stated that the government would have fully complied if the Leader of the Islamic Revolution had ordered against negotiations. He stressed adherence to the Supreme Leader's guidance.

headlines.title: Gharibabadi: Doha talks focused on US violations of MoU by US, frozen asstes
headlines.snippet: Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister for Legal and International Affairs stated that talks in Doha focused on US violations of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding, particularly concerning the ceasefire in Lebanon. A working group was formed to monitor implementation.

headlines.title: Armed forces on high alert as Iran prepares for Ayatollah Khamenei’s funeral
headlines.snippet: Iran’s armed forces have increased their presence across borders to ensure security during funeral ceremonies for the Supreme Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei.

headlines.title: The Strait of Hormuz pain has been China’s gain, The Asia Group says
headlines.snippet: According to The Asia Group, China has benefited most from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, as it has unevenly impacted Asian economies reliant on imports.

headlines.title: US, Iranian officials hold indirect, lower-level talks in Doha, source says
headlines.snippet: US and Iranian officials are engaged in indirect, lower-level technical talks in Doha, facilitated by Qatari and Pakistani mediators. US special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner met with Qatar's prime minister.
